How fresh is this? Full rebuild end-of-day after the close on the 2026-06-25 data, then refreshed hourly during US market hours (prices, intraday options-flow signals; the AI reads stay on the EOD batch). Stock & technical screeners update once a day (EOD), options-flow screeners (Intraday ~30 min) refresh during the session. Insider feeds are daily, 13F is quarterly. Each card shows its own refresh cadence and data date. Screeners are ordered highest data confidence first.

★ Cross-screener confluence

CONFLUENCE
🧠 Tickers flagged by 2 or more independent screeners within one day; validated combos ranked first, everything else is context.
Backtested combos first (May-2026 sweeps + pairs battery), then today's coherent overlaps. Trade-ready funnel: best_trades_today →

🎈 Blow-off top detector NEW

BLOWOFF_DETECTOR
🧠 Spots parabolic tops about to roll over on the top-sector winners — Greenwood-Shleifer-You 2-year rallies, Edwards-Magee parabolic, gap exhaustion, island reversals and LPPLS variance acceleration. Only the max-drawdown-backtested signals carry weight; the academic heuristics confirm. GO / NO GO on naked puts comes from the live Polygon options chain.

Read
Top blow-off candidate today: MMM (score 4, target ~$158.95 (DD20d -5.0%)).

2 ticker(s) fire at least ONE Bonferroni-validated backtested signal (25y, 50,109 obs, p<0.001). 0 stack ≥3 points = high conviction.

Methodology: the backtest-validated signals measure max drawdown over 20d (not the mean forward return — masked by rebounds in a bull market). The academic heuristics (Greenwood-Shleifer-You, Edwards-Magee, gap, island, LPPLS) stay displayed for context but do not survive Bonferroni on their own — they only confirm.
How to read this
Backtest-validated signals (25 years, 91 tickers, 50,109 observations, p<0.001):
• Two-red parabolic (weight 3) — 2 red days after parabolic. DD 20d -11.55%, lift +20pp, N=302.
• Stoch cross<80 + parabolic (weight 2) — DD 20d -9.65%, N=148.
• Rally + red on volume (weight 2) — rally 10%+ then red day. DD 20d -7.23%, N=93.
• Shooting star / BB fail / Bearish engulf (weight 1) — DD 20d -3 to -5%.

Options decision = multi-factor composite 0-100 (IV Rank · IV-HV · term struct · skew 25Δ · implied/DD ratio · liquidity). Score ≥70 + liquidity OK → 🟢 GO ATM Put. Score 50-70 → 🟡 GO LIGHT OTM 25Δ. <50 or liquidity KO → 🔴 NO GO + reason.

Global composite REJECT on its own (mean forward return bull-biased). Individual signals VALID on the max-DD metric.

🧠 Detects institutional intent via unusual options sweeps confirmed by next-day OI delta, EMA 8/21 structure, and low-OI anomaly scoring.
Construction
Unusual options sweep + next-day OI delta confirmation (OPENING vs CLOSING) + EMA8/21 structure + low-OI anomaly. Score includes OI Delta, premium $, signal persistence, EMA alignment.
Top picks: $COIN $CORZ $TEVA
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayPatternTypeStrikePremiumOI verdictOI ΔPersistEMAStreakNet 20d

2CORZ ↑59 Watch$28.02 +0.83%BULLISH SWEEPCALL33$101KOPENING188167PULLBACK33
3TEVA ↑50 Watch$34.13 -0.25%BULLISH SWEEPCALL33$270KOPENING161258STRONG22
4TLT ↑50 Watch$87.58 +0.22%BULLISH SWEEPCALL75$605KNOT_FOUND—97STRONG99
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Starts from the prior day's UNUSUAL signals, then checks T+1 OI Delta
  • OI Delta: OPENING (+25), LIKELY_OPENING (+15), CLOSING (-50), NEUTRAL (+5)
  • 20d flow persistence: net≥5 → +30, ≥3 → +20, ≥2 → +10, ≤-2 → -15; streak bonus ≥3
  • Multi-leg spread detected: -30; Low-OI anomaly: +15
  • EMA STRONG +10 / PULLBACK +5 / BROKEN -10; quality HIGH +5 / LOW -5 (min mcap $500M)
  • Archives only non-CLOSING signals AND score ≥ 50
Score
clamp(0,100, OI_delta + persistence + spread + low_oi + ema + quality). CLOSING dropped. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 With only 5 resolved trades in 90 days against the 20-trade minimum, the live 100% win rate is statistically hollow and cannot confirm or contradict the backtest's 68.1% figure - the monthly cohorts (100% win rate, avg 20d return of 5,546% in May and 3,611% in April) suggest options-premium dynamics but the sample is too thin to weight heavily.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
HALF SIZEinsufficient data (5 resolved, need ≥20)
Last 90 days: 289 signals captured · 5 resolved at +20d · WR 100% · avg +200.0% · vs SPY +4.6%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 285 closed · WR TB 100% · 0% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

COIN tops the batch at 60 and CORZ sits just behind at 59, both flagging bullish sweeps in crypto-adjacent names that could catch a risk-on move. TEVA and TLT both land at 50, adding pharmaceutical and rates exposure but at the weakest score the screener printed today. The distribution is thin and low-ceiling: 4 signals, max 60, median 50, zero clearing 65 or 80, which means no signal crossed the threshold where the backtest edge is sharpest. A NEUTRAL macro regime (56.93) offers no directional tailwind. Take COIN first if you must act, small size, and treat this as a monitor-only day rather than a high-conviction entry.

🧠 Ranks premium-selling candidates by IV30-HV30 spread with Sinclair sector tilt, rewarding utilities structurally and penalizing tech.
Construction
IV30 vs HV30 spread + sector bias (Sinclair Table 4.7: Utilities +31%, Tech -8%) + value factors (P/E, RoE, mcap). Score 0-100 ranks premium-selling candidates.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Liquid universe: market cap ≥ $2B, volume ≥ 500K
  • VP spread (IV Rank vs HV30) - weight 35%
  • Sinclair sector bias (Utilities +31% … Tech -8%) - weight 25%
  • Value factors (P/E, RoE, RoA, D/E, mcap) - weight 25%; IV Rank - weight 15%
  • Garbage-stock bonus (high P/E, low RoE) up to +15
Score
clamp(0,100, vp×0.35 + sector×0.25 + value×0.25 + ivr×0.15 + garbage_bonus). LONG (sell puts), top 30.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live 90-day tape (WR 57.2%, avg +2.1% vs SPY essentially flat at -0.02%) and improving monthly cohort averages (May +4.3%, April +6.7%) confirm rather than contradict the backtest Sharpe of 2.34, with the OOS test ratio of 2.13 versus train 1.53 holding up in real money - the key number is that 2.1-point live edge over SPY on 500 resolved trades.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

PCG, EXC, and XEL lead at 76, all utilities, which is exactly where Sinclair's framework assigns a +31% structural edge, so the sector concentration is a feature, not a quirk. PM at 73 is the one non-utility worth attention, offering portfolio diversification without stepping outside Signal tier. The score distribution is tightly compressed: median 68, max only 76, zero names above 80, and 42 of 46 above 65, meaning differentiation within today's batch is thin and rank order carries limited conviction. A NEUTRAL macro regime (56.93) provides no directional tailwind for premium sellers but no headwind either, making this a tape-neutral setup where realized vol staying below implied is the entire bet. PCG first at highest score, moderate conviction only given the absence of any pattern confirmation and the compressed score range.

🧠 Identifies tickers where options are priced coherently with realized vol, targeting the MID VRP tertile to avoid catalyst-bloated or disbelief-discounted names.
Construction
Longs tickers in the MID tertile of the variance risk premium (IV vs realized): options priced coherently with realized vol, neither too cheap (LOW = market disbelief) nor too rich (HIGH = catalyst already priced).

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Reads the variance_premium_daily table on the latest day
  • Keeps only the MID tertile (OOS sweet spot: neither LOW cheap nor HIGH catalyst-priced)
  • Sorted by vrp_proxy ascending, top 30
Score
max(50, 100 − rank×50/n) within the MID tertile. LONG.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

TGT leads at a perfect 100 with Very strong tier, making it the cleanest expression of the screener's logic today. ISSC (99) and FNV (97) follow closely, though both lack pattern data, so the case rests entirely on the VRP score. The distribution is top-heavy: 13 of 30 signals clear 80, and 22 of 30 clear 65, with a median of 75, meaning the batch has real concentration at the high end rather than a flat spread. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 56.93 is neither a headwind nor a catalyst, which suits a vol-structure play that does not need directional macro support. Start with TGT at high conviction given the max score, treat ISSC as a secondary add pending any fundamental check on the name.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • For each ticker on the latest options_daily_flow day
  • Trigger: put_volume ≥ 3.0× the 30d moving average of put_volume
  • AND put_volume ≥ 100 contracts
Score
min(100, put_ratio × 20) where put_ratio = put_volume / 30d average. LONG (contrarian: hedging = bottom near).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

Every single one of the 50 signals scores exactly 100, with all 50 sitting at or above the 80 threshold and the median pinned at the ceiling - the screener offers zero intra-batch differentiation today, so pick order is effectively arbitrary. BTG is the most defensible name, a gold miner where a put-heavy hedge unwind thesis aligns with commodity positioning, and DIG, a leveraged energy ETF, is worth a look if energy flows are rotating. ARQQ adds speculative quantum exposure but carries no pattern context whatsoever, same as every other name in the batch. The NEUTRAL macro regime (score 56.93) provides neither a tailwind nor a clear headwind for this contrarian setup. Given the scoring collapse, overall conviction is low - BTG first, small, with a tight stop, purely on the commodity hedge narrative.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Excluded if pulled back > 25% below the 52-week high
  • Uptrend: 90d slope > +0.20%/day, 60d slope > +0.25%/day, 90d R² > 0.70, price > EMA50 and EMA200
  • Pullback in progress: 30d slope < +0.15%/day, 10d slope < +0.05%/day, RSI(14) between 40 and 65 (sweet spot 45-55)
  • Healthy pullback: 10d/30d volume < 0.80, ATR contracting, no down gaps, Fibonacci level 38/50/62%, <15% below 52w high
  • Recovery: higher lows, rising 5d volume, bullish RSI divergence
Score
Uptrend (max 30) + Pullback (max 25) + Healthy (max 25) + Recovery (max 30). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Live 90-day data confirms the core backtest thesis with 64.9% win rate and avg +1.85% beating SPY by 3.8 pts, but the monthly cohorts contradict any bullish acceleration, with win rates compressing from 67% in March to 51.7% in May and avg returns falling from +4.95% to +1.07%, that 15-point win rate drop being the key number that argues for reduced sizing in the current environment.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
FULL SIZE OKWR 65% · avg +1.9% · beats SPY -2.0% by +3.8 pts
Last 90 days: 620 signals captured · 493 resolved at +20d · WR 65% · avg +1.9% · vs SPY -2.0%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 494 closed · WR TB 38% · 23% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

AVNS, GBTG, and JAZZ score 100 in the Very strong tier, all printing the LAUNCH pattern, with EBAY, OGN, and WHWK close behind at 95. Every one of the 50 signals scores 80 or above with a median of 85, making this a fully top-heavy batch with zero mid-tier gradation. A NEUTRAL macro score of 56.93 is neither tailwind nor headwind for pullback reversals. The live Very strong tier track record is the problem, showing only 62.5% win rate and a negative average of -3.03% across 8 resolved trades, far worse than the Signal tier at 72.5% win rate and +2.42% avg. JAZZ is the lead name on score, but the tier-level underperformance in live data argues for small size and no conviction rush.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch of 27 signals is the most compressed distribution possible: every single name scores 91 or 94, median 91, with zero names below 80 - there is no internal ranking signal here, just a uniform cluster at the top. At the 94 tier, ASAN, BBAI, MNDY, NIO, PDD, and RR all tie with no pattern data to separate them; ASAN and MNDY are domestic software names with cleaner fundamental profiles than NIO (China EV, currency and regulatory overhang) or BBAI (thin float, speculative). The NEUTRAL macro regime (score 56.93) does not actively suppress mean-reversion plays but provides no tailwind either. This screener is explicitly a basket construct at 1-2% per name, not a single-name trigger, so treat the whole cohort as the position - if forced to sequence entries, ASAN first at low conviction, with the reminder that the edge lives in the spread across names, not any individual one.

🧠 Identifies vol-of-vol exhaustion via VVIX percentile and VIX term structure to flag optimal short-volatility entry points.
Construction
VVIX percentile + VIX term structure (VIX3M/VIX) + FOMC proximity + weekend effect. Detects 'vol-of-vol' climax (sell-vol opportunities at the top of fear).
Top picks: $SPY
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayVerdictComposite
1SPY ↑61 Watch$737.42 +0.57%SELL VOL1.33
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Market-level signal archived under SPY (Sinclair composite)
  • VVIX: ≥90th pctile → SELL VOL (+2), ≤10th → BUY VOL (-2)
  • Term structure VIX3M/VIX < 1.0 contango → +1; VIX regime 13-19 sweet +1, >24 danger -1
  • Weekend (Friday) +0.5; FOMC 3d ahead +0.5; Hurst/timing optional
Score
sum of sub-scores (~-6 to +6). ≥3.5 STRONG SELL VOL … ≤-3.5 STRONG BUY VOL. Archived score = clamp(0,100,(total+6)/12×100).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape earns a KEEP verdict but the margin is razor thin, 68% win rate and avg +1.8% beating SPY's +1.7% by essentially zero, while the monthly drift from April's 100% win rate on four trades down to May's 65% at +1.1% average suggests the edge is compressing, not expanding.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is as thin as it gets: one name, SPY at a score of 61 in Watch tier with no pattern attached. The score distribution is degenerate, max equals median at 61, nothing clears 65, nothing clears 80. This screener is built to catch climax fear, and a NEUTRAL macro regime (score 56.93) with a single borderline signal says the tape is not delivering that setup today. SPY LONG at Watch conviction is not a screener call worth acting on; it is the screener telling you to wait.

🧠 Detects institutional options sweeps at 5x normal volume with $50K+ premium, filtered to calm, non-extended stocks to avoid chasing late signals.
Construction
Stocks with options volume > 5× mean(20d) + premium > $50K + Vol/OI > 5. V3 filters now applied at write time: keeps only calm (vol<1.5× avg) and non-extended (±5% from EMA20) tickers, drops LATE-timing chasing signals.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Per-contract filters: Vol/OI > 3.0×, volume > 500, premium > $100K, contract OI ≥ 10, chain OI ≥ 5000
  • Expiration 7-365 DTE; deep ITM excluded
  • Pattern: delta>0.7 → HEDGE, call → BULLISH SWEEP, put → BEARISH SWEEP
  • Timing: LATE (-15) if chasing a >3% move, EARLY (+10)
  • V3 hard filters (before archive): volume < 1.5× the 20d average, |price-EMA20| < 5%, timing ≠ LATE
Score
Vol/OI (max 30) + log premium (max 30) + OTM (max 20) + delta (max 20) + timing (-15/+10) + IV compression (IVRank<15 +15). Archived if ≥50.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape shows 100% win rate on 24 resolved trades (avg +188.0% vs SPY -0.4%, a +188.4-point spread), and monthly cohorts for March through May 2026 confirm 100% win rates, but only 24 of 620 total signals have resolved (3.9% resolution), so the key number is sample size, not win rate, and the backtest's 61% win rate on 72 trades with a Bonferroni p of 0.097 remains the more honest baseline until resolution catches up.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

EWY leads the batch at score 89 (Strong) but carries a BEARISH SWEEP pattern against a LONG direction tag, a contradiction that signals possible hedging of Korean equity exposure rather than a clean directional bet - treat with skepticism before sizing. TQQQ at 75 is the cleanest name: BULLISH SWEEP aligns with LONG direction, and leveraged QQQ flow in a NEUTRAL macro regime (56.93) is at least internally consistent. WULF at 70 (Signal, BULLISH SWEEP, LONG) adds a crypto-adjacent speculative layer if you want secondary exposure. The score distribution is top-heavy and thin: one name above 80, four above 65, median at 56, with the back half of the eight-name list clustered in the 56-59 Watch range. A NEUTRAL macro regime neither supports nor undermines the batch, but the EWY direction conflict and the weak tail make this an average day. First preference is TQQQ on signal clarity, low conviction.

Construction
Classifies 11 GICS sector ETFs into 8 categories: EARLY_ENTRY, RIDING, LATE_CHASE, EXHAUSTED, DECELERATING, NEW_DOWN, DEAD, NEUTRAL. Uses strength + acceleration + RS crossover vs SPY.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

XLE and XLC are the clearest names today, both scoring 80 in the Strong tier, though neither carries a pattern tag so the precise GICS category driving the signal is absent. XLK and XLI sit at 65 in Signal tier, a step below but still above the median. The distribution is modestly top-heavy: 4 of 11 names at or above 80, 6 at or above 65, median at 65, which reads as a decent but not emphatic batch. With macro at NEUTRAL (56.93), the tape offers no directional tailwind, and the defensive representation in the Watch tier (XLRE, XLP, XLV) muddies the rotation narrative. First look goes to XLE over XLC given energy's typical sensitivity to a NEUTRAL-to-RISK-ON macro shift, but conviction is moderate given missing pattern data across the board.

🧠 Flags stocks down 40%+ from 52-week highs where capitulation volume and a reversal candle mark exhaustion selling, targeting mean-reversion within 15 days.
Construction
Deep oversold (≥-40% from 52w high) with capitulation volume + reversal candle. Score combines drawdown depth, ATR contraction, volume spike, and proximity to 52w low. ATR-based stop 2.5×, target 3.0×, max hold 15 days.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Downtrend: 90d slope < -0.20%/day, 60d slope < -0.25%/day, accelerating decline
  • Oversold: RSI(14) < 30, price < -15% below EMA20, < -30% below EMA50, Bollinger %B < 0, <20% above 52w low
  • Capitulation: 10d/30d volume > 1.50, ATR ratio > 1.5, ≥7 red days out of 10, ≥2 down gaps, reversal bar
  • Signs of Strength: slope deceleration, higher lows, 1-5 day bounce off the low, bullish RSI divergence
  • Persistence bonus: signal seen ≥10 times in 15d = +20, ≥5 times = +10
Score
Downtrend (max 30) + Oversold (max 25) + Capitulation (max 45) + SOS (max 35) + Persistence (0/10/20). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The recent tape contradicts the backtest sharply: live 90-day win rate of 44.7% versus the backtest's 63.3% is a 19-point gap, and the Strong tier's live average of -10% at a 28.6% win rate is the single most damning number here, though the monthly cohort trend is stabilizing, with average returns recovering from -9.5% in January to +4.5% in April and +3.4% in May, suggesting the screener is regime-sensitive rather than structurally broken.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

LBRDA at a perfect 100 and GTM plus NIQ both at 95 are the three names that earn attention today, all Very strong tier, all printing BREAKOUT patterns off deep oversold reads. WIX at 90 is next in line and rounds out a notably top-heavy batch where 28 of 50 signals score 80 or above and the median sits at 81, so supply of high-conviction setups is genuine today, not scattered. The NEUTRAL macro regime at 56.93 is neither a tailwind nor a headwind, which is roughly what this screener needs: not a risk-off panic, not a ripping tape that makes the entry chase. One caution is that the three Strong-tier names lower on the list (BYRN, MENS, ADCT at 89/89/86) sit in a live-tracking tier that has destroyed value, so ignore them for now. LBRDA first, at moderate conviction, size to the 2.5x ATR stop.

Data confidence HALF SIZE · Sharpe 0.73 Stocks-on-the-Move, walk-forward Sharpe 0.73 (+45.6% vs SPY); trend-quality factor, not a per-trade trigger Full methodology →
🧠 Ranks trend quality as annualized 90d regression slope times R-squared, gated on price above MA100, gaps below 15 percent, and SPY above MA200.
Construction
Andreas Clenow 'Stocks on the Move' (Trading Evolved ch.12). Score = annualized exponential-regression slope (90d) × R², filtered on price > MA100 and max recent gap < 15%. Buys only when SPY > MA200 (regime gate). ATR-based sizing (10 bps risk/position).

HUM is the only name worth attention today, scoring 100 (Very strong) in a field where the next closest, PANW at 61, does not even clear the Signal tier. Data confidence HALF SIZE doctrine 8-step: MARGINAL at h=5d (Sharpe 0.5-0.6), REJECT at h>=10d; 60d academic drift not reproduced Full methodology →
🧠 Detects post-earnings price continuation by combining EPS surprise magnitude, gap size, gap retention, and analyst revision momentum to score stocks likely to drift higher over 1-3 weeks.
Construction
Post-earnings continuation: positive EPS surprise + gap up + holds gap into 1-3 weeks. Score uses surprise magnitude, gap size, post-gap action, and analyst revision activity.
Top picks: $BBCP $BKE
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySurpriseConsistencyPost-earn ret
1BBCP ↑90●●● Very strong$11.36 +1.75%500.0%437.6%
2BKE ↑70● Signal$44.39 -0.40%24.7%4-3.7%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • POSITIVE direction (EPS beat), surprise ≥ 5.0%
  • Days since earnings in [1, 20]
  • Consistency (beats in the last 4 quarters) ≥ 2
  • Price ≥ $5, 20d avg volume ≥ 500K; final inclusion if score ≥ 45
Score
surprise (≥20 +40, ≥10 +30, ≥5 +20) + consistency (≥4 +25, ≥3 +15, ≥2 +8) + window (≤3d +20, ≤7 +15, ≤14 +10) + drift (>3% +15, >0 +10). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥45. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape at 62.8% win rate and +5.1% average return against a 0.07% SPY baseline over the same window confirms a real edge and directly contradicts the backtest's MARGINAL verdict at h=5d, with the May 2026 cohort (n=621, 62.0% WR, +4.5% avg) showing the pattern is holding in recent conditions, though April's six-trade sample at 50% is too thin to read.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Two names cleared today, making this one of the thinnest batches possible. BBCP leads at 90 (Very strong, PRIMED), the only name above 80 and the clear priority given the tier's 90-day average of 5.1% and 64.5% win rate. BKE prints 70 (Signal, BUY), a workable setup but historically the Signal tier produces a slightly lower win rate at 61.3%, and its 2.9% average in the Strong tier (the adjacent cohort) is the weakest in the stack. Score distribution is about as top-heavy as it gets with a two-signal day and a 20-point gap between names. Macro sits at NEUTRAL (56.93), offering no meaningful tailwind or headwind, which makes gap-hold discipline especially important. Take BBCP first at moderate conviction, size conservatively given the thin tape.

Data confidence HALF SIZE backtest_murphy_intermarket doctrine 8-step: 13 buckets MARGINAL_INVERSE Bonferroni-significant (+6 to +14% SPY at 60-120d after intermarket correlation breaks) Full methodology →
🧠 Fires a contrarian SPY long when two or more major intermarket pair correlations break by at least 2 sigma over 7 days, with severity scaling to the count of broken pairs.
Construction
Counts intermarket correlation breaks (|z| >= 2 sigma over 7d) across major pairs from intermarket_corr_history; >= 2 broken pairs fires a contrarian BUY on SPY, severity scales with the count.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Polygon universe: ≥ 260d history, avg dollar-volume ≥ $200K, price ≥ $1
  • Entry: daily close crosses above the daily EMA(200)
  • Mandatory weekly filter: weekly close > weekly EMA(50)
  • Window: cross within the last 5 trading days
Score
60 + freshness×30 (= (5−bars since cross)/5) + 10 if weekly filter active, capped at 100. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90d live tape (WR 64.5%, avg +4.2% vs. SPY -1.0%, verdict KEEP) confirms the screener earns its place, and the May 2026 cohort at 63.4% WR aligns cleanly, but the key number is the tier inversion: 'Signal' tier posts 84.2% WR against 'Very strong' at 60.0%, meaning today's all-'Very strong' batch carries the lowest expected edge of the three live cohorts.

So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

The score distribution is fully compressed: all 50 signals print at 100, median 100, so the score adds zero intra-batch discrimination today and tier is the only differentiator. AGCO and ACNT are the most recognizable names in the 'Very strong' cohort, both at score 100, but pattern fields are null across every pick, leaving the raw crossover as the sole signal. No 'Signal' tier names appear, which matters because 90d live data shows 'Signal' tier posts 84.2% WR and +8.83% avg versus 60% WR and +2.76% avg for 'Very strong', meaning today's batch occupies the weakest-performing live cohort. Macro sits at NEUTRAL (56.93), neither a structural tailwind nor a headwind. If forced to rank, AGCO first on sector specificity, but conviction is limited given the tier mismatch, absent pattern context, and undifferentiated scoring.

🧠 Flags high-short-interest stocks (>20% float) with cost-to-borrow spikes and a technical breakout above a key moving average, targeting forced short-cover momentum.
Construction
High short interest (>20% float) + cost-to-borrow spike + technical breakout above key MA. Score blends SI %, days-to-cover, CTB, and price momentum.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $3, 20d avg volume > 300K, market cap $200M-$50B
  • Mandatory gate: short interest ≥ 10% of float AND days-to-cover ≥ 3.0
  • Short interest: ≥30% float (+40), ≥20% (+30), ≥15% (+20)
  • Days to cover: ≥8d (+25), ≥5d (+15), ≥3d (+8)
  • Catalyst (not a falling knife): price > SMA50 AND 20d return > +5% (+20)
  • Volume building 10d/30d > 1.3 (+10), RSI(14) 40-70 not extended (+5)
Score
sum of components 0-100. Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥45. LONG.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

BEAM leads at 95 with a PRIMED pattern and is the only Very strong name at the top of the score range, making it the first name to size. CPB at 90 is the second Very strong signal and adds a defensive consumer angle to a batch that skews speculative. The distribution is clustered high, with 47 of 50 signals at 65 or above and a median of 70, meaning there is very little tail drag today. A NEUTRAL macro regime (56.93) is not a tailwind but does not block squeeze dynamics, which are stock-specific by nature. Start BEAM at moderate conviction, treat CPB as secondary, and leave the 85-tier names as watch-list only until price confirms.

🧠 Identifies stocks with abnormal relative volume across three lookback windows multiplied by multi-horizon price momentum, betting on volume-led breakout persistence.
Construction
Reproduces layer #5 of the @kerberos007 thread ('Volume leader screen from RUT-1000'): stocks ranked by relative volume (V/10d, V/30d, V/90d avg) × multi-horizon price momentum (1d/5d/20d/90d). Filters 20d momentum > 0 and price > 5. Score 0-100 = percentile rank of the leader score across the eligible universe.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

MU and SNDK both print 100 with Very strong tier, a semiconductor pair worth watching together given shared supply-chain drivers, but pattern data is null for all names so the signal is raw rank with no confirming structure. KYMR at 100 is a small-cap biotech, high idiosyncratic risk with no pattern anchor. The score distribution is fully compressed: all 50 signals sit at 80 or above, median 96, meaning the screener offers zero rank differentiation today and the entire universe is tied at the ceiling. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 56.93 neither confirms nor kills a momentum lean. Given that the Very strong tier in live tracking posts a 30% win rate and -8.47% average 20-day return, acting on any of today's top scores at full size is contraindicated. If forced to pick one name, MU has the most liquid options market for defined-risk positioning, but conviction is low.

🧠 Detects large OTM options block trades on quiet stocks, confirmed by next-day OI delta to isolate new institutional positioning.
Construction
Large options block trades (>$200K premium, 1-10 DTE, OTM) on quiet stocks. Confirms next-day with OI delta to verify NEW position vs closing. Score weights total premium, vol/OI ratio, OI confirmation.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Stealth institutional accumulation over 60d of OHLCV (optionable universe)
  • Block day: dollar-volume z-score ≥ 1.5σ vs the 60d baseline (excluding the last 5 days)
  • Stealth: low price impact = low |return| / σ_returns
  • Persistence: number of the last 5 days with dollar-volume z > 1.0; up-day bonus
Score
clamp(vol_z/4)×40 + stealth×30 + (persist_5d/5)×20 + (10 if up_day). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape (55% win rate, avg +2.8% vs SPY +0.1%) broadly confirms the screener has an edge worth keeping, and the May and April monthly cohorts at 55% and 53% win rates with positive average returns run in the same direction - though the Very Strong tier's tiny sample of 7 trades and the unrigorous backtest (no block-bootstrap, Sharpe cited at ~3.4 without DSR adjustment) mean the +2.6 point SPY beat is the key number to anchor on, not the headline Sharpe.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

CWAN leads the batch at 93, the only Very Strong name today, and its score stands well clear of the pack - that gap alone makes it the first-look name. GGR at 88 is the cleanest runner-up, followed by a cluster of four Strong names (KEMQ, GNOM, SMQ, KW, VSCO, AIFU) all stacked between 80 and 83 with no distinguishing pattern data attached to any of them. Score distribution across 28 signals is moderately top-heavy: 8 names at 80-plus, 16 at 65-plus, median at 67, which is a decent-quality day but not exceptional. Macro sits at NEUTRAL (56.93), consistent with the usual uneven hit rate for short-dated flow plays, so this is not a high-conviction tape. Preference is CWAN first at moderate conviction given its score separation; treat the 80-83 cluster as lower-priority watches until pattern data or further confirmation arrives.

🧠 Catches post-earnings drift continuation by entering on a weekly close above the prior week's high after a red-candle pullback within four weeks of an earnings gap.
Construction
Weekly red-candle pullback within first 4 weeks post-earnings gap, then weekly close above prior week's high. Identifies high-quality continuation entries on positive earnings reactions.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Opening gap on one of the last 3 bars: 5.0% ≤ gap < 10.0%
  • Volume confirmation: gap-day volume / 20d average > 1.5
  • Dollar-volume ≥ $50M
Score
gap_pct × 15 (not bounded 0-100 → shown here rescaled, top pick = 100). LONG.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Three signals, all LONG, with ACA printing a perfect score of 100 at Very strong tier, the cleanest setup in today's batch. 🧠 Detects stocks printing excess high bars at the top of their value area with rejection tails at least 1.8x ATR, flagging overextended supply zones prone to mean reversion lower.

Data confidence STARTER backtest_dalton_excess 2026-04 (500 tickers 2022-2026): raw excess = coin flip (WR 51%); WITH extreme-location + tail>=1.8xATR filter +3.18% @10d, N=74 only, no block bootstrap Full methodology →
🧠 Detects institutional excess reversals at Market Profile value-area extremes using rejection-tail bars at least 1.8x ATR, targeting mean-reversion after failed breakouts.
Construction
Market Profile on daily bars (Dalton, Mind Over Markets): rejection-tail bars >= 1.8x ATR formed at value-area extremes (location <= 30% or >= 70%), the only configuration that backtested positive.

tail_atr × 50 (tail_atr = wick / ATR14). LONG.

Data confidence STARTER prod-cabled KVO+FORCE combo, paper KEPT WR 67% vs SKIPPED 46% over 60d; no formal Bonferroni verdict yet Full methodology →
🧠 Filters SMART_FLOW signals by excluding trades within 3 days of KVO or Force Index crossovers, using volume divergence as a negative gate.
Construction
SMART_FLOW signals filtered by skipping any trade with KVO_SIGNAL_CROSS or FORCE_INDEX_FLIP active ±3 days. Volume rules act as a negative filter (combo OR-skip).
Top picks: $TLT
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayPatternTypeStrikePremiumOI verdictOI ΔPersistEMAStreakNet 20d
1TLT ↑50 Watch$87.58 +0.22%BULLISH SWEEPCALL75$605KNOT_FOUND—97STRONG99
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Reads SMART_FLOW signals (2-day lookback)
  • Negative filter: SKIP if KVO_SIGNAL_CROSS OR FORCE_INDEX_FLIP volume rule active ±3 trading days
  • T+1 OI Delta filter: SKIP if call_volume(T+1)/call_volume(T) < 0.50
  • Keeps only signals that pass both filters
Score
original SMART_FLOW score, unchanged (no re-scoring). LONG.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

One signal today: TLT at score 50, Watch tier, flagged as a bullish sweep long. A score of 50 sits at the floor of the Watch band and carries no conviction on its own. The distribution is as thin as it gets - one name, zero signals above 65, nothing close to actionable strength. NEUTRAL macro regime at 56.93 does not contradict a TLT long directionally, since rate softness fits a bonds-up thesis, but the screener is not confirming it with force. TLT first if you must touch something, but this batch is weak and waiting a session costs nothing.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • FOMO momentum required: 5d perf ≥ +3.0% AND 4-week perf ≥ +8.0%
  • Confirmed breakout: close > 2% above resistance (60d high) + day volume > 1.5× the 20d average (CONFIRMED +20, else WEAK +5)
  • Regime gate: not archived if VIX > 20 (OOS decay -0.49 Sharpe at VIX 20-25)
Score
perf_5d×0.3 + perf_4w×0.3 + perf_13w×0.2 + return_1y×0.2 + breakout_points (+20/+5). Not bounded 0-100 → shown here rescaled (top pick = 100). LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Live tracking overrules the REJECT backtest here - 90-day resolved trades show 53% win rate and +5.1% avg against SPY's +0.16%, and the tier breakdown confirms the score filter works, with Strong-tier names averaging +15.5% against Watch-tier names averaging -7.6%, which is the key number that makes tier discipline non-negotiable.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

MUU is the only name worth discussing, scoring a perfect 100 as the sole Very Strong signal in a 38-name batch where the median score sits at 12 and only one ticker clears 65. That score cliff is the story today - this is an extremely top-heavy, nearly empty batch. AGL at 62 lands in Watch territory with no pattern data to anchor conviction. The macro regime is NEUTRAL at 56.93, which does not invalidate breakout setups but provides no tailwind. Lead with MUU only, at moderate conviction, given the total absence of a second corroborating name at any reasonable score.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Parabolic trend: 90d slope > +0.25%/day, acceleration (30d > 60d > 90d)
  • Over-extension: RSI(14) > 70, price > +15% above EMA20, > +30% above EMA50, Bollinger %B > 1.0
  • Exhaustion: volume divergence (price up, volume down), ATR ratio > 1.5, ≥7 green days out of 10, ≥2 up gaps
  • Signs of Weakness: 1-5 day rollover off the high, bearish RSI divergence
Score
Trend (max 30) + Extension (max 25) + Exhaustion (max 40) + SOW (max 35). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. SHORT.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Three names hit max score 80 today - BTQ, DFTX, and NVCT - all flagging TRIGGER, the highest-conviction pattern in this screener. That is also where the danger is: Strong-tier signals historically carry a 20% win rate and average -20.79% return from the short side, meaning the top-scored names are empirically the worst performers here. Score distribution is compressed and top-heavy, with 48 of 50 signals at 65 or above and only 3 reaching the 80 ceiling, leaving little dispersion to calibrate off. The NEUTRAL macro regime at 56.93 provides no directional tailwind for short setups. If forced to act, Signal-tier names like AMC or ABSI at score 75 are preferable to the top trio, but conviction must stay low given the rejected backtest and the historically lethal Strong-tier track record.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

The score distribution makes differentiation impossible: all 50 signals print at 99-100, meaning the ranker has fully compressed to its ceiling and is providing no cross-sectional signal within the batch. RIOT and OSCR are the most recognizable names at score 100, Very Strong, but that designation is meaningless when every single name shares it. No patterns are populated for any pick, stripping away whatever catalyst logic the model was supposed to surface. The macro regime is NEUTRAL at 56.93, a setting where a degraded momentum signal carries even less conviction. The backtest is formally REJECTED - the +15%/yr gross collapses to -0.8%/yr alpha at p=0.25, and the headline +467% was a leveraged-ETF artifact. Do not size any of these names off this screen today.

🧠 Identifies weekly RSI(14) sub-30 oversold equities and scores daily crossover, candlestick reversal, and zone confluence for a contrarian long entry.
Construction
Contrarian RSI(14) reversal: HARD GATE weekly RSI(14) < 30 on the last completed week (coded 2026-06-09), then daily RSI oversold/crossover + candlestick reversal + demethod zone scoring.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • HARD GATE: weekly RSI(14) < 30 on the last completed week (fail-closed, added 2026-06-09)
  • RSI oversold: RSI(14) ≤ 30 (+15), ≤ 20 extreme (+10), ≥4 days under 30 (+5)
  • Reversal candlestick (hammer/doji/piercing/engulfing) on T-0 to T-2
  • RSI crossover: RSI back above 30 today or yesterday
  • Demethod zone: swing-low cluster (3% tol) + proximity to EMA20 (<3% +5) or EMA50 (+3)
  • Volume spike ≥2.0× (+5) + VIX %B regime (BTFD_BOOST +15 to DANGER -10)
Score
RSI Oversold (max 30) + Candlestick (max 25) + Crossover (max 20) + Demethod+EMA (max 15) + Volume (max 10) + Regime (-10 to +15), bounded 0-100. Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live tape contradicts any edge case: 90-day resolved WR of 69% sounds acceptable but the average return of +3.7% lags SPY by 2.9 points, the Signal tier is the only sub-group that beats the index at +6.6% average across 58 trades, and the May cohort collapsed to +0.21% average confirming that the deteriorating monthly trend, not the backtest, is the current reality.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

BR at 68 leads the batch, the only Signal-tier name with a score that clears the top of today's range, followed closely by WLK at 67 and DKL at 66, the only three names above 65 in a 31-signal field. The score distribution is compressed and low-ceiling: median 55, max 68, zero names above 80, which means the screener is not generating high-conviction setups today. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 56.93 does not actively suppress the trade but offers no tailwind for a beaten-down name to re-rate quickly. Take BR first if you act at all, Signal tier, modest size, given the ceiling on scores and the weak batch character.

🧠 Detects unusual volume, premium, and OI delta on crypto-linked ETFs to front-run spot crypto momentum via flow intensity scoring.
Construction
Tracks unusual flow on crypto-linked ETFs (BITO, IBIT, FBTC, ETHE, GBTC, etc.) - volume vs mean, premium %, OI delta. Score weights flow intensity + spot crypto correlation.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Fixed 12-ticker universe: spot ETFs (IBIT/FBTC/ETHE/BITO) + equity proxies (MSTR/COIN/MARA/RIOT/…)
  • Filters: price > $2, 21d median USD ADV > $5M
  • Uptrend gate: SMA50 > SMA200 AND price > SMA50
  • Pullback: price between -8% and +1% vs SMA20; RSI(14) 35-55; RS vs IBIT 20d > 0
Score
Uptrend (max 35) + Pullback (max 25) + RSI (max 20) + RS (max 10) + Volume (max 10) ± regime, bounded 0-100. Archived if ≥35. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The headline 90-day verdict of KEEP (WR 65%, avg +2.0%, beating SPY by 2 pts) is almost entirely a Watch-tier artifact - Watch carries 62 of 71 resolved trades at 72.6% WR and +5.1% avg, while the Strong tier (1 trade, -13.2%) and Signal tier (8 trades, WR 12.5%, avg -20.2%) that make up today's entire batch have been consistently destructive, and May 2026 cohort depth (n=66) confirms this is not a thin-sample quirk.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

CLSK leads at 95 with a TRIGGER pattern and is the only name in the Very Strong tier, making it the first look. CORZ and HUT both score 85 and share the Strong TRIGGER tag, offering a secondary pair trade on the mining complex. The score distribution is top-heavy and compressed, with a median of 85 and all five signals clearing 65, suggesting a cluster of correlated miners rather than a broad opportunity set. The macro regime sits at NEUTRAL (56.93), which does not actively support a high-conviction crypto run, and the backtest on this screener carries a hard REJECT with 0 of 16 signals surviving adversarial validation post-spot-ETF 2024. If playing at all, CLSK first at low size only, and the tier data below makes a strong case for patience.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

IFRA is the only name clearing the Strong tier at a score of 80, making it the clear first look - infrastructure as an emerging theme is credible against ongoing fiscal spend narratives and a NEUTRAL macro regime (score 56.93) that does not punish rate-sensitive real assets. ARKG at 77 and XLV at 75 represent a healthcare cluster worth watching together, though the absence of any pattern flag on either reduces conviction. The score distribution is top-heavy but thin: one name at 80, ten more between 65 and 79, and a median of 55 across 50 signals, meaning the bulk of the batch is noise. A NEUTRAL regime fits infrastructure and defensive health better than speculative genomics, so IFRA is the first call at moderate conviction, with ARKG a secondary watch only if biotech tape improves.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Eight names hit the maximum score of 100 in the Very Strong tier today: BL, CELH, HDV, MAZE, QDEL, RNA, SRTY, and TNDM. CELH and QDEL stand out as the most tactically interesting given their histories of sharp momentum moves, though the screener supplies no pattern data to differentiate any of the eight. SRTY is a leveraged inverse small-cap ETF flagged LONG, which is either a genuine signal on small-cap deterioration or a construction artifact worth flagging before trading. Score distribution is top-heavy: 12 of 50 signals sit at 80 or above, median lands at 70, and 41 of 50 clear 65, so the batch is concentrated rather than broad. Macro regime is NEUTRAL at 56.93, neither a headwind nor a tailwind. Conviction is low until the screener clears a formal backtest; if forced to act, CELH first on score and sector momentum, one-third size.

🧠 Aggregates quarterly SEC 13F filings from 20 hedge funds to score stocks by fresh institutional conviction, exploiting the herding signal embedded in overlapping new and increased positions.
Construction
Quarterly SEC 13F-HR filings from 20 top hedge funds via EDGAR. Whale Score: +20/NEW (cap 60), +10/INCREASED (cap 30), -5/CLOSED, -3/DECREASED. Stale 60-90 days by nature (quarterly).

ASML at 97 and FB at 93 are the only two names in the Very Strong tier and together account for the ceiling of a batch where just 2 of 50 signals clear 80 and the median sits at 55, making the distribution sharply top-heavy with a long, weak tail. Pattern data is null across all picks, so the thesis rests entirely on the whale score with no technical overlay. The macro regime is NEUTRAL at 56.93, which neither amplifies nor undercuts a 13F approach that is already 60 to 90 days stale by construction, and a crowded-semis/tech complex in NEUTRAL tape historically provides limited incremental edge from this signal alone. First preference is ASML at low conviction given its score lead, but the staleness risk is real and position sizing should reflect that.

🧠 Identifies stocks within LATE_CHASE and RIDING sectors that have lagged the broader sector move, scoring them on sector relative strength times individual under-performance to flag catch-up candidates.
Construction
Within LATE_CHASE / RIDING sectors, identifies the lagging individual stocks that haven't yet caught the sector move. Score = sector relative strength × stock under-performance.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is weak and should be treated as noise. All 8 named picks - SR, CEG, OKLO, KEP, SPH, SBET, ABTC, BMNR - land at exactly 50 with no pattern data attached, meaning the screener has not differentiated a single name above the floor. The full 23-signal distribution has a median of 50 and a max of 50, with zero names clearing 65 or 80, which is a flatlined output rather than a ranked opportunity set. CEG and OKLO carry the most recognizable fundamental narratives in nuclear power, but the screener itself offers no incremental signal to justify acting on either ahead of the others. NEUTRAL macro at 56.93 adds no tailwind. Pass on this batch entirely and wait for the score distribution to develop conviction above 65 before sizing into any name here.

Data confidence Starter · untested · no rigorous backtest yet, queued for the untested-screeners battery. Methodology →
🧠 Ranks non-financial, non-utility equities by EV/EBIT ascending, surfacing the cheapest operating-earnings values in the investable universe.
Construction
Tobias Carlisle's Acquirer's Multiple = Enterprise Value / operating earnings (EBIT). Universe is market cap > $200M, excludes Financial Services and Utilities (EV distorted), drops negative operating earnings. Ranks the universe by multiple ascending and surfaces the cheapest names (score 100 = cheapest of the day).

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $200M, excludes Financial Services & Utilities (EV distorted)
  • Acquirer's Multiple = Enterprise Value / operating earnings (EBIT, operating income)
  • Operating earnings from Polygon financials (operating_income / EBIT); EV from yfinance else proxy market cap + non-current liabilities
  • Drops negative operating earnings; ranks the universe by multiple ascending (cheapest first)
Score
score = inverse rank scaled 0-100 (cheapest of the day = 100). LONG (deep value selection).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

BZ at score 100 and WRD at 97 are the two names the screen is most confident in today, both flagged Very strong with no disqualifying pattern data to parse. KROS at 93 is the eyebrow-raiser: a clinical-stage biotech clearing a deep-value EV/EBIT filter suggests either depressed operating losses have turned or the denominator is being read generously, so verify the EBIT figure before touching it. GPC at 90 is the cleanest industrial read in the batch, a durable compounder trading at a discount the screen finds extreme. Score distribution is moderately top-heavy, with 7 of 16 names above 80 and a median of 73, meaning roughly two-thirds of the universe clears a useful threshold. Macro regime sits at NEUTRAL (56.93), neither a tailwind nor a headwind for value rotation. First look goes to GPC over BZ or WRD given earnings-quality certainty, at modest conviction until the backtest closes.

🧠 Detects coordinated insider accumulation via SEC Form 4 cluster filings, weighting C-suite rank and dollar size to identify conviction buying before price moves.
Construction
SEC Form 4 cluster buys: 3+ insiders buying within 30 days, with C-suite or director rank weighted higher. Score uses cluster size, total $ value, insider rank, and recency.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • At least 1 insider buy on the ticker
  • Cluster if ≥ 3 unique insiders within a 14-day window
  • C-level detected if title is CEO/CFO/COO/CTO/President/Chairman
  • Average transaction-size tiers: ≥$200K or ≥$100K
Score
buy base (≥2 +15, 1 +5) + cluster +25 + C-level +20 + avg (≥200K +15, ≥100K +8), cap 60. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live 90-day tape confirms a real edge - WR 60% and avg +3.8% beats SPY by +2.6 points across 354 resolved trades - but the April cohort (WR 44%, avg +1.2%) shows the screener degrades sharply in choppy tape before recovering to WR 65% and avg +4.3% in May, meaning regime sensitivity is the key risk the aggregate number obscures.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is weak on differentiation: all 14 signals score exactly 60 with zero names clearing 65 or 80, making score-based ranking impossible. ADSK and FISV are the most credible names given their market cap and liquidity - large-cap insiders opening wallets carries more signal weight than a micro-cap cluster, and the dollar-value weighting in this screener's construction should favor them. BRID and GUTS sit at the micro-cap end where cluster definitions are looser and noise runs high. The score distribution is entirely flat at the median of 60, which is a thin, undifferentiated batch. Macro is NEUTRAL at 56.93, neither a headwind nor a catalyst. Take ADSK first on name quality alone, but hold conviction at minimum given the complete score compression.

🧠 Catches fake breakouts above the Value Area High that close back inside the value area, trapping longs for a short entry.
Construction
Fake breakout above VAH followed by a close back inside the value area = bull trap, longs trapped, SHORT signal.
Top picks: $AAXJ $AVEM $ARTY
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTrap typeStopTarget POC
1AAXJ ↓75● Signal$120.08 +2.46%BULL_TRAP118.4297.83
2AVEM ↓75● Signal$96.94 +2.28%BULL_TRAP96.8081.69
3ARTY ↓70● Signal$76.64 +3.63%BULL_TRAP75.8371.57
4ASX ↓70● Signal$42.40 +2.56%BULL_TRAP41.3435.16
5ADI ↓65● Signal$430.80 +4.27%BULL_TRAP431.49416.05
6AIRR ↓65● Signal$132.62 +1.64%BULL_TRAP130.81124.76
7AIS ↓65● Signal$85.60 +6.64%BULL_TRAP82.4280.90
8ANET ↓65● Signal$167.32 +3.45%BULL_TRAP167.51144.39
9ARW ↓65● Signal$230.11 +2.38%BULL_TRAP230.42189.83
10ASHR ↓65● Signal$36.83 +2.70%BULL_TRAP36.3634.81
11ALAB ↓60 Watch$419.74 +4.96%BULL_TRAP416.67340.37
12AME ↓60 Watch$238.67 +1.40%BULL_TRAP238.90232.78
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • BULL_TRAP (SHORT): close[N-1] > VAH then close[N] < VAH (failed bullish breakout rejected)
  • fake_dist and reclaim_dist in [0.2, 2.0] ATR; total pct_move ≤ 12%
  • score ≥ 55 required
Score
50 base + volume_ratio (>1.5 +15) + fake_dist (>1.0 +10) + reclaim_dist (>0.5 +10) + rejection_wick (>0.5 +10) + cleanness (+5), bounded 0-100. SHORT.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 With only 5 resolved trades against 64 signals the tape is noise, not evidence - Watch-tier names run 0% on 4 trades while the sole Signal-tier name shows 100%, a split that is statistically meaningless, and the May 2026 cohort posted zero wins across 2 trades at a mean of -4.55%, so neither the tier breakdown nor the monthly trend confirms or contradicts the backtest thesis, which itself has no trades to show.

🧠 Ranks macro ETFs by Antonacci dual momentum - requires positive 12-month absolute return and top relative rank versus peers, holding leaders.

🧠 Ranks tickers by 20-day relative strength within leading sectors, combining breadth and cluster position to surface sector-rotation leaders.
Construction
Top relative-strength tickers within bullish sectors. Score = relative strength vs SPY (20d) + sector breadth + cluster ranking.
Top picks: $AAT $AAMI $ABCL
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySectorRet 1mRet 3m
1AAT ↑82●● Strong$24.67 +1.23%Real Estate19.0%31.5%

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $300M
  • Step 1: bullish sectors = sector ETF (XLK/XLV/…) with 20d return > 0
  • Step 2: score the stocks in each bullish sector, top 10/sector
Score
Momentum 0-100 = 1M Ret (30%) + 3M Ret (30%) + RS vs SPY 3M (20%) + Volume Trend (20%), normalized components. ≥70 strong. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape and the May cohort both confirm the screener works: 55% win rate, +2.9% avg versus SPY at -1.4% over 124 resolved trades, and May printed +4.7% avg on 58.6% wins, but the key number is the tier split - Signal at +9.8% carries the whole average while Strong at -3.8% is a drag, meaning score alone does not determine quality here.

Construction
Detects retail-driven euphoria: retail call volume spike, Reddit mention surge, parabolic price, no earnings catalyst. Score combines social attention z-score + call/put + price stretch.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked

  • Tiers: ≥1.00 FOMO, ≥0.75 EUPHORIA, ≥0.50 GREED (scan threshold 0.50)

clamp(0,100, call_vol_oi_ratio × 100). SHORT (fade the retail crowd).

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=25 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags tickers where IV30 minus HV20 z-score exceeds 1.5 standard deviations, identifying elevated fear premium as a candidate for options premium selling.
Construction
IV30 − HV20 spread spike on individual tickers (z-score > 1.5σ on 90d). Detects unusual fear premium - candidates for premium selling.
Top picks: $NFLX $ALNY $CMCSA
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30HV30RatioZone
1NFLX ↓64 Watch$71.28 -0.78%46%28%1.62FEAR
2ALNY ↓61 Watch$292.31 -0.59%49%32%1.55FEAR
3CMCSA ↓61 Watch$22.68 +0.15%40%26%1.53FEAR
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • ATM IV30 / realized HV30 ratio (price ≥ $10, ≥35 days of closes)
  • Zones: ≥2.0 EXTREME_FEAR, ≥1.5 FEAR, ≥1.2 ELEVATED, 1.0-1.2 NORMAL, <1.0 COMPLACENT
  • Archives only FEAR and EXTREME_FEAR
Score
min(100, ratio × 40) where ratio = IV30/HV30. NEUTRAL.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=10 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags 25-delta put/call skew spikes above the 90-day baseline, treating elevated hedging demand as a contrarian mean-reversion setup when the underlying is trading calmly.
Construction
25-delta skew (put IV − call IV) spike vs 90d baseline. Bearish skew = hedging demand spike, contrarian read when stock is calm.
Top picks: $GOOGL $MU
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30IV rankSkew 25dPlaybook
1GOOGL ↓100●●● Very strong$336.37 -2.58%151%94—SELL CALL/PUT (IV rich)
2MU ↑64 Watch$1251.20 +19.33%38%1—BUY PREMIUM (IV cheap)
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Cross-section scan of the watchlist (large caps + ETFs)
  • Skew_Z = (skew_25d − basket median) / basket std
  • IV_Rich_Z = (IV_HV_spread − basket median) / basket std
  • Composite = IV_Rich_Z×1.0 + Skew_Z×0.5; archived if |Composite| ≥ 1.5
Score
int(|Composite| × 20). SHORT vol if Composite>0 (IV rich), LONG vol if <0.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape shows 58% win rate and +11.1% average beating SPY by 9.4 points, and May's cohort improved further to 67.3% WR, both confirming the screener has live edge - but the tier breakdown reveals a dangerous inversion where Very strong signals (16.7% WR, -4.3% avg) dramatically underperform Watch signals (75% WR, +18.9% avg), meaning the score ranking is inversely predictive of actual outcome, the single most important number being that 58-point win-rate gap between the top and bottom tiers.

EOD
Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=0 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects value-area false breakouts where a close outside VAL/VAH is reclaimed next session, trapping committed directional players and triggering a mean-reversion squeeze.
Construction
Kerberos Apr-2020 thesis adapted to daily bars: a close below VAL (fake breakdown) followed by a reclaim next day = bear trap, shorts trapped, LONG signal. Mirror logic above VAH for bull traps.
Top picks: $ARLO $AVDE
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTrap typeStopTarget POCATR
1ARLO ↑55 Watch$12.80 +3.06%BEAR_TRAP12.4712.940.56
2AVDE ↑55 Watch$89.25 +0.88%BEAR_TRAP88.9690.941.18
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • BEAR_TRAP (LONG): close[N-1] < VAL then close[N] > VAL (failed bearish breakout reclaimed)
  • fake_dist and reclaim_dist in [0.2, 2.0] ATR; total pct_move ≤ 12%
  • score ≥ 55 required
Score
50 base + volume_ratio (>1.5 +15) + fake_dist (>1.0 +10) + reclaim_dist (>0.5 +10) + rejection_wick (>0.5 +10) + cleanness (+5), bounded 0-100. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape shows 77.8% win rate and a 14.88% average 20-day return across 9 resolved trades versus a near-zero SPY baseline, which is directionally consistent with the thesis but the verdict is correctly held at WATCH because 9 observations is well below the 20-trade minimum, and the single May 2026 cohort at 85.7% WR adds color without adding statistical weight.

🧠 Detects stocks where put IV exceeds call IV above the 90th percentile, flagging unusual put demand as a potential directional signal.
Construction
Bearish IV skew bias: put IV >> call IV at extreme percentiles (>90th). Detects unusual put demand.
Top picks: $AVGO
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySkew ptsIV30IV rankVerdict
1AVGO ↑100●●● Very strong$385.95 +1.01%16.7468.7%97BEAR_SKEW_EXTREME
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • 25Δ skew (30 DTE target) > +5 vol points (puts overpriced)
  • Earnings in 0-7 days (catalyst near)
  • IV Rank > 60 (high premium = fear)
  • Verdict: skew>10pts + IVRank>80 EXTREME, skew>7pts HIGH, else MILD
Score
clamp(50,100, 50 + skew_25d_pts × 3). LONG (BTFD contrarian: fear overpriced).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Seven resolved trades show a 71.4% win rate but an average 20-day return of -0.33% against SPY at +1.43%, a gap almost entirely explained by one Strong-tier blowup at -88.77%, while the May cohort's +14.57% average reversed April's -20.21% drag, suggesting tier quality is the key number to track - but the sample needs to reach 20 resolved trades before any of this confirms or contradicts a backtest that does not yet exist.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=1 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags pre-earnings names where straddle IV underprices Yang-Zhang HV20, creating a long-vol edge before the event.
Construction
Pre-earnings (3-14d to event) with straddle IV < expected move from Yang-Zhang HV20. Identifies when implied vol underprices realized expectation.
Top picks: $MU
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySectionDTEExpectedPlay
1MU ↓50 Watch$1251.20 +19.33%REVERSAL1DOWN (reversal)BUY PUT (reversal)
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Market cap ≥ $1B
  • PRE_IV_RAMP: earnings DTE 7-14d AND IV Rank < 50 (premium not yet expensive)
  • POST_PEAD: days_since earnings 0-5d
  • REVERSAL: earnings ≤ 2d AND pre-earnings drift ≥ 3%
Score
by section: PRE_IV_RAMP (DTE + IV rank + consistency + mcap, max 100), POST_PEAD (surprise + consistency + days + drift, max 100), REVERSAL (|drift| + DTE, max 60).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is a single name: MU scores 50, sitting at Watch tier with no pattern resolved and a SHORT direction flag, which runs counter to the screener's long-vol premise and warrants caution about signal integrity. The score distribution is as thin as it gets - one signal, max and median both at 50, nothing at or above 65. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 56.93 neither supports nor opposes the setup. With no signal clearing the 65 threshold and the lone name flagged SHORT against a long-vol construct, conviction is low - hold MU on watch only and do not size until the pattern field populates and direction aligns with the screener logic.

#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30IV rankHV20Rule
1VXX ↓86●● Strong$22.99 -2.95%56%257%RULE_1
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Vol-decay basket (UVXY, VXX, SVXY, SDS, QID, SQQQ, FAZ, TZA)
  • Rule 1 (3 conditions required): IV30 < 100% AND IV Rank < 10 AND VIX3M/VIX > 1.0 (contango)
  • 2/3 conditions → inactive (score 55)
Score
if 3/3 active: min(100, 85 + max(0, 3−iv_rank)). NEUTRAL.

Altman

EOD
Data confidence No backtest stats yet (N < 30 or not run) · starter size, discretionary read. Methodology & all-screeners comparison →
🧠 ALTMAN screens for Altman Z-score distress signals to flag bankruptcy risk or recovery setups in US equities.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $300M, excludes Financial Services & Utilities (model not applicable)
  • Altman Z = 1.2×(WC/TA) + 1.4×(RE/TA) + 3.3×(EBIT/TA) + 0.6×(MktCap/TL) + 1.0×(Rev/TA)
  • Financials from Polygon (else yfinance)
  • Output filter: keeps only Z < 2.5 (grey + distress)
Score
score = the Z-Score itself. Zones: Z>2.99 SAFE, 1.81-2.99 GREY, <1.81 DISTRESS, <1.0 critical. LONG (risk filter).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is empty. Zero top picks, one signal with a score of zero, and no names reaching the 65 or 80 threshold. The score distribution is as thin as it gets - a single observation at the floor. Macro regime is NEUTRAL at 56.93, which is neither a tailwind nor a headwind, but it is irrelevant when the screener produces nothing actionable. No conviction, no preferred name - pass on today's output entirely.

🧠 Measures index implied vol versus constituent implied vol to identify whether options markets favor stock-pickers or macro-driven correlation trades.
Construction
Index implied vol versus the average implied vol of its constituents. High dispersion = stock-pickers market, low = macro-driven.

🧠 Detects equity fear/complacency via VIX3M minus VIX spread; contango signals calm, backwardation signals stress.
Construction
VIX futures basis = VIX3M minus VIX. Contango = complacency, backwardation = fear. See Volatility Desk for the live read.

Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: price > $10 (shortable)
  • Analyst gate: recommendationMean ≥ 3.0 (Hold or worse)
  • Fundamental gate: EVA Proxy Score ≤ 40 (low quality)
  • EVA Proxy 0-100 = 5 blocks of 20 pts: Profitability, Growth, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow, Valuation
Score
score = EVA Proxy (0-100, lower = worse). Top 50 sorted ascending (worst first). SHORT.

🧠 No formal validity verdict yet.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $3, 20d avg volume > 200K, market cap > $50M
  • Per-bar metrics (last 5): relative volume vs SMA20, normalized spread, close position within the range
  • Bullish patterns: No Supply, Testing, Stopping Volume, Absorption, Falling Pressure, Failed Down Move
  • Bearish patterns: No Demand, Up-Thrust, Buying Climax, Effort To Rise Failed
  • 20-bar accumulation/distribution background + Relative Strength vs SPY 20d
Score
best-pattern priority (No Supply 3 → Buying Climax 6) + background alignment (+2/-1) + RS vs SPY alignment (+1). Top 40. LONG or SHORT depending on pattern.
Not included on this page (by design)
These Google-Sheet tabs are not per-ticker screeners, so they have no card here: ETF_RANKING (asset-class ranking), CPR_RANK (Camarilla pivot assist), SECTOR_STRENGTH / SECTOR_BREADTH / SECTOR_ROTATION (sector-level gauges, see the macro cockpit), SKEW_VELOCITY / VOLATILITY_DASHBOARD / VVIX market panel (market-level vol gauges, see the cockpit), STRUCTURAL_FLOWS (OpEx / quarter-end calendar), QQQ_PAIRS / PAIR_ZSCORE (pair-trading lab, REJECT OOS), NEWS_SENTIMENT (newsletter prep data), MULTI_STRATEGY / META_SCREENER (aggregators, superseded by Best Trades), plus journal / portfolio / content tabs. Macro composites (BTFD index, COT, Kerberos PCR) live on the cockpit and macro pages.
Generated by build_screeners_deep.py · sources: signal_archive, whale_13f, openinsider, hrhr_ranked, options_flow_shortlist, emergence_history, sector_acceleration/laggards
Data confidence scale = a discretionary sizing guide (full / half / starter), never a permission verdict · stats from screener_validity + block-bootstrap v12
Sparklines = ~6 months daily close · AI reads regenerate at the EOD build, page refreshes hourly during US market hours
